title: The Great Gatsby
description: A story of the mysteriously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his obsessive love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan set against the backdrop of the Jazz Age and the American Dream.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
description: The unforgettable story of racial injustice and the destruction of innocence in 1930s Alabama as seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch and her principled lawyer father Atticus.
In a totalitarian superstate where Big Brother watches your every move and the Thought Police punish independent thinking Winston Smith dares to fall in love and keep a private diary.

title: Brave New World
description: A chillingly prescient novel set in a future where citizens are genetically engineered happiness is chemically enforced and individuality is considered a social disease.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
description: Teenager Holden Caulfield narrates his disillusionment with the adult world after being expelled from prep school in this landmark coming-of-age novel that defined a generation.

title: Pride and Prejudice
description: The witty and wise Elizabeth Bennet navigates love and social expectations in Regency England as she sparns pride and prejudice to find her perfect match in the wealthy Mr. Darcy.

title: The Alchemist
description: A young Andalusian shepherd named Santiago journeys across the Egyptian desert in search of hidden treasure and discovers that the real treasure lies within the journey itself.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: Harry Potter discovers he is a wizard on his eleventh birthday and enters the magical world of Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry where he uncovers the mystery of his past.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
description: Harry's second year at Hogwarts is marked by dark ominous warnings and a terrifying monster hidden in the school's secret chamber that threatens to petrify all Muggle-borns.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
description: Harry discovers that a notorious prisoner who escaped from Azkaban has a dangerous connection to his past in this darker third installment of the beloved fantasy series.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
description: Young hobbit Frodo Baggins inherits a magic ring and embarks on a perilous quest with his Fellowship to destroy it in the fires of Mount Doom and save Middle-earth from darkness.

title: The Hobbit
description: The comfortable homebody Bilbo Baggins is swept into an epic quest with a band of dwarves and a wizard to reclaim a mountain stronghold from the dragon Smaug.

title: A Game of Thrones
description: Noble families fight for control of the Iron Throne of the Seven Kingdoms while ancient threats stir beyond the frozen Wall and a young girl discovers she has a destiny of fire.

title: The Name of the Wind
description: Kvothe the legendary wizard outlaw and musician recounts the tale of how he came from nothing to become the most notorious man in the world in this breathtaking first-person narrative.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
description: In a world where ash falls from the sky and mists rule the night a crew of thieves plans the ultimate heist to overthrow an immortal god-emperor using a magic system based on swallowed metals.

title: American Gods
description: Shadow Moon is released from prison and hired as a bodyguard for the mysterious Mr. Wednesday who is recruiting old gods for a war against the new gods of technology and media.

title: Good Omens
description: An angel and a demon who have grown fond of Earth team up to prevent the coming Apocalypse but their young Antichrist has been accidentally switched at birth and nobody can find him.

title: Dune
description: On the desert planet Arrakis young Paul Atreides must survive political treachery and the harsh environment while fulfilling his destiny among the native Fremen in this cornerstone of science fiction.

title: Foundation
description: Psychohistorian Hari Seldon foresees the collapse of the Galactic Empire and creates a plan to shorten the dark age to follow by establishing a Foundation of knowledge at the galaxy's edge.

title: Ender's Game
description: Genius child Andrew Ender Wiggin is trained in zero-gravity battle games at a military school orbiting Earth to prepare for an alien invasion nobody else seems capable of stopping.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: Moments before Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass Arthur Dent is swept into space by his friend Ford Prefect a researcher for the eponymous guidebook.

title: Neuromancer
description: Washed-up hacker Case is hired for one last job in the neon-drenched streets of Chiba City in this groundbreaking novel that invented the concept of cyberspace and defined cyberpunk fiction.

title: Snow Crash
description: In a near-future America divided into private franchise city-states hacker-slash-pizza-delivery-ninja Hiro Protagonist races to stop a memetic virus that crashes hackers' brains.

title: The Martian
description: After a sudden storm forces his crew to evacuate astronaut Mark Watney is stranded alone on Mars with limited supplies and must use his ingenuity to survive while NASA races to rescue him.

title: Project Hail Mary
description: A lone astronaut wakes up with amnesia on a spacecraft millions of miles from Earth and must piece together his memories to discover his mission could be humanity's only hope for survival.

title: The Three-Body Problem
description: During China's Cultural Revolution a secret military project sends signals into space attracting the attention of an alien civilization planning to invade Earth four centuries later.

title: Fahrenheit 451
description: In a future America books are outlawed and burned by firemen Guy Montag is a fireman who begins to question the book-burning culture after meeting a curious young neighbor.

title: Recursion
description: NYPD detective Barry Sutton investigates a strange new disease called False Memory Syndrome while neuroscientist Helena Smith works on a device to preserve memories with terrifying consequences.

title: Dark Matter
description: Physicist Jason Dessen is kidnapped and wakes up in an alternate version of his life where he is famous and single and must find his way back through parallel realities to his real family.

title: Ready Player One
description: In 2045 the world has retreated into the OASIS a virtual reality universe and teenager Wade Watts embarks on a world-wide treasure hunt with life-or-death stakes for a dead billionaire's fortune.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
description: Journalist Mikael Blomkvist and hacker Lisbeth Salander team up to investigate a 40-year-old disappearance from within a wealthy Swedish family uncovering a dark web of violence and corruption.

title: Gone Girl
description: When Nick Dunne's wife Amy goes missing on their fifth anniversary the investigation reveals layer upon layer of deception in a masterfully constructed psychological thriller about marriage and identity.

title: The Da Vinci Code
description: Harvard professor Robert Langdon finds himself in the middle of a murder investigation in the Louvre that leads to a trail of clues hidden in the works of Leonardo da Vinci.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
description: FBI trainee Clarice Starling must seek the help of the brilliant imprisoned cannibal Dr. Hannibal Lecter to catch another serial killer known as Buffalo Bill who skins his victims.

title: And Then There Were None
description: Ten strangers are lured to a remote island and one by one are murdered by an unknown killer in this masterfully plotted mystery that is the world's best-selling crime novel.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
description: When a passenger is found dead on a stranded train Hercule Poirot must investigate a crime with twelve suspects all of whom had both motive and opportunity in this classic whodunit.

title: The Shining
description: Jack Torrance takes his family to be caretakers of an off-season mountain hotel but the hotel's dark history unleashes supernatural forces that drive him to madness and threaten his family.

title: It
description: Seven outcasts face an ancient evil entity that preys on children's fears in the small town of Derry Maine returning as adults to fulfill a childhood oath to destroy it once and for all.

title: The Hunger Games
description: In a dystopian future sixteen-year-old Katniss Everdeen volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised fight-to-the-death competition as an act of rebellion against the Capitol.

title: Divergent
description: In a future Chicago society is divided into factions based on virtues but sixteen-year-old Tris Prior discovers she is Divergent and uncovers a conspiracy that threatens her world.

title: The Maze Runner
description: Thomas wakes up in a lift surrounded by boys in a mysterious glade enclosed by an enormous ever-changing maze with no memory of his past and a desperate need to find an exit.

title: The Notebook
description: An elderly man reads a love story from a notebook to a woman with memory loss — the story of Noah Calhoun and Allie Nelson whose summer romance was torn apart by class differences and war.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
description: Sixteen-year-old cancer patient Hazel Grace Lancaster falls in love with charming fellow survivor Augustus Waters who takes her on a journey to Amsterdam to meet her favorite author.

title: Me Before You
description: Small-town girl Louisa Clark becomes caregiver to Will Traynor a young man left paralyzed by an accident and the two form an unexpected bond that will change both their lives forever.

title: It Ends with Us
description: Lily Bloom moves to Boston builds her dream flower shop and falls for charming neurosurgeon Ryle in a story that sensitively explores the complexities of toxic relationships and breaking cycles.

title: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief
description: Twelve-year-old Percy Jackson discovers he is the son of a Greek god and must go on a cross-country quest to find Zeus's stolen lightning bolt and prevent a war between the Olympians.

title: A Brief History of Time
description: Stephen Hawking takes non-specialist readers on a tour of the cosmos explaining the Big Bang black holes the nature of time and the quest for a grand unified theory of everything.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
description: Yuval Noah Harari traces the 70000-year history of our species from the earliest hominids through the Cognitive Revolution Agricultural Revolution Industrial Revolution and into the future.

title: Atomic Habits
description: James Clear presents a proven framework for improving every day using the science of tiny habits and small changes that over time compound into remarkable results.

title: Thinking Fast and Slow
description: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman reveals the two systems that drive the way we think — fast intuitive emotional System 1 and slow deliberative logical System 2 — and their biases.

title: The Power of Now
description: Eckhart Tolle teaches that the key to enlightenment and peace is to silence the mind and live fully in the present moment drawing on Buddhism and Advaita Vedanta traditions.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
description: Stephen Covey presents a principle-centered approach for solving personal and professional problems through a paradigm shift toward alignment with natural principles of effectiveness.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
description: Dale Carnegie's timeless guide to interacting with people covers fundamental techniques for building relationships handling people making them like you winning them to your way of thinking.

title: Deep Work
description: Cal Newport argues that the ability to focus without distraction on cognitively demanding tasks is a superpower and provides rules for cultivating this skill in an age of social media.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
description: Mark Manson argues that improving our lives requires not positivity but learning to choose our struggles wisely and focusing our limited attention only on what is truly important.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
description: Robert Kiyosaki contrasts the financial philosophies of his educated poor father and his rich entrepreneur friend to argue that financial education and passive income are the path to wealth.

title: Zero to One
description: Peter Thiel argues that creating true value means doing something that has never been done before going from zero to one — and provides contrarian thinking for building the future.

title: The Lean Startup
description: Eric Ries presents a scientific approach to building and managing startups using validated learning rapid experimentation and iterative product development to reduce waste and risk.

title: Outliers
description: Malcolm Gladwell examines the factors behind extraordinary success — from opportunity to practice time to cultural legacy — challenging the myth of the self-made individual.

title: The Tipping Point
description: Malcolm Gladwell explores how little things can make a big difference by examining the moment when an idea trend or social behavior crosses a threshold and spreads like wildfire.

title: Blink
description: Malcolm Gladwell explores the power of thinking without thinking examining how snap judgments and first impressions are made and when to trust or distrust them in everyday life.

title: Educated
description: Tara Westover recounts her remarkable journey from growing up in a survivalist family in rural Idaho with no formal education to earning a PhD from Cambridge University.

title: Becoming
description: In her memoir Michelle Obama chronicles the experiences that have shaped her from her childhood on the South Side of Chicago to her years as First Lady of the United States.

title: Meditations
description: The private journal of Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius offers timeless Stoic philosophy on how to live virtuously maintain inner peace amid chaos and fulfil one's duty without complaint.

title: The Art of War
description: Sun Tzu's ancient treatise on military strategy is the most influential work on strategy and tactics ever written with applications in business leadership and conflict resolution.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
description: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl describes his experiences as a prisoner in Nazi concentration camps and his psychotherapeutic method logotherapy which posits that meaning is our primary motivation.

title: Siddhartha
description: A young Brahmin named Siddhartha leaves his privileged home in ancient India on a spiritual quest for enlightenment through sensuality asceticism and ultimately self-discovery near a sacred river.

title: Crime and Punishment
description: A brilliant but poverty-stricken student in St. Petersburg murders a pawnbroker convinced he is above moral law only to be consumed by guilt psychological torment and eventual confession.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
description: The tumultuous lives of four brothers and their father form the backdrop for Dostoevsky's masterpiece exploring faith doubt morality murder and the existence of God in 19th-century Russia.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
description: The multigenerational story of the Buendia family in the mythical town of Macondo blends the magical with the mundane in this foundational work of Latin American magical realism.

title: Don Quixote
description: The aging nobleman Alonso Quijano renames himself Don Quixote and sets off on horseback with his squire Sancho Panza on a series of comic misadventures inspired by chivalric romances.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
description: Edmond Dantes is wrongfully imprisoned in a fortress dungeon but he escapes acquires wealth and transforms himself into the mysterious Count to exact perfect revenge on his enemies.

title: Anna Karenina
description: Aristocrat Anna Karenina abandons her husband and son for a dashing cavalry officer in an affair that society will not forgive in Tolstoy's masterpiece about love passion and moral duty.

title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
description: A 19th-century American factory worker is transported to Arthurian England where he uses his modern knowledge to introduce technology and democracy to medieval society with satirical results.

title: Frankenstein
description: Victor Frankenstein obsessively creates life from dead tissue only to be horrified by his creation and abandon it — setting off a chain of tragedy in this first science fiction novel.

title: Dracula
description: Solicitor Jonathan Harker travels to Transylvania and falls into the clutches of Count Dracula who travels to England to spread his vampiric curse in this defining vampire novel.

title: The Sun Also Rises
description: American expatriates travel from Paris to Pamplona for the running of the bulls in this quintessential novel of the Lost Generation exploring disillusionment love and masculinity after WWI.

title: A Farewell to Arms
description: American ambulance driver Frederic Henry falls in love with British nurse Catherine Barkley during World War I in Hemingway's devastating semi-autobiographical novel of love and loss in wartime Italy.

title: Catch-22
description: U.S. Army Air Forces bombardier Yossarian desperately tries to escape flying combat missions by claiming insanity but faces the paradoxical military bureaucracy rule known as Catch-22.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
description: Billy Pilgrim has come unstuck in time and experiences his life non-linearly including his survival of the firebombing of Dresden and alien abduction by the time-seeing Tralfamadorians.

title: The Stranger
description: Meursault a detached Algerian French man shoots an Arab in a moment of inexplicable violence then faces trial more for his emotional indifference at his mother's funeral than for the murder.

title: The Kite Runner
description: Amir a wealthy boy from Kabul must atone for his greatest childhood sin — betraying his servant and closest friend Hassan — in a journey back to Taliban-ruled Afghanistan.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
description: In the near-future theocratic Republic of Gilead fertile women called Handmaids are forced to bear children for powerful men in this chilling feminist dystopia.

title: A Little Life
description: Four college friends navigate life in New York City over several decades but the story centers on Jude St. Francis whose traumatic past slowly reveals itself in devastating detail.

title: The Midnight Library
description: Nora Seed stands between life and death in a magical library where every book represents a different life she could have lived and she must decide which one is worth living fully.

title: Tomorrow and Tomorrow and Tomorrow
description: Sam and Sadie meet in a hospital as children and decades later collaborate on video games forming a bond that is more than friendship and less than romance but utterly transformative.

title: Normal People
description: Connell and Marianne grow up in the same small town in Ireland but only discover their deep connection after reaching Trinity College Dublin in this sharp novel about love class and miscommunication.

title: Little Fires Everywhere
description: In picture-perfect Shaker Heights Ohio the orderly life of the Richardson family is upended by the arrival of a free-spirited artist and her daughter setting in motion a story about secrets and identity.

title: The Road
description: A father and his young son walk alone through burned America south toward the sea carrying nothing but a pistol to protect themselves as they encounter the depravity of the new world.

title: Klara and the Sun
description: Klara an Artificial Friend observes the world from a store window and when she is chosen as a companion for ill teenager Josie questions arise about consciousness love and what it means to be human.

title: A Man Called Ove
description: A grumpy antisocial man who has given up on life is slowly drawn back into his community when a young family crashes into his life bringing warmth chaos and unexpected friendship.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
description: Kya the Marsh Girl raises herself in the marshes of North Carolina after being abandoned by her family and when a local man is found dead she becomes the prime suspect.

title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
description: Aging Hollywood icon Evelyn Hugo grants an exclusive interview to an unknown journalist who must unravel the secrets of her seven marriages and discover why she was chosen.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: After Earth is demolished for a hyperspace bypass Arthur Dent and Ford Prefect hitchhike across the galaxy encountering depressed robots bureaucratic aliens and the answer to life the universe and everything.
